meta.description: All seasonal campaign emails — Spring (4), Fall (4), Snow (3) = 11 emails total

emails.internal_name: Spring — Your Yard Survived Winter. Barely.
emails.subject_a: The snow's melting. Here's what it did to your yard.
emails.subject_b: {{first_name}}, your yard needs this before spring hits
emails.preview_text: Winter did a number on Madison yards this year. Here's the fix.
emails.headline: Winter Did a Number on Your Yard. We Fix That.
emails.subheadline: Spring cleanup season is here — and early birds save 15%. Here's what your yard needs right now.
emails.hero_image_alt: Split before and after — snow-damaged Madison yard vs post-spring-cleanup yard
emails.body_html:
Madison winters don't just cover your yard in snow — they quietly destroy it. Here are the 5 things winter did to your property while you were inside:

  1. Snow mold — Those gray or pink patches hiding under melting snow? That's fungus eating your grass. It thrives under snowpack and kills turf fast if left untreated.
  2. Salt damage — Road salt and sidewalk deicer splashed onto your lawn all winter. It dehydrates grass roots and leaves brown, dead strips along driveways and walkways.
  3. Debris buildup — Branches, leaves, and whatever the wind dragged in. All of it is smothering your grass right now, blocking sunlight and trapping moisture.
  4. Compacted soil — Months of freeze-thaw cycles crushed your soil. Compacted soil means roots can't breathe, water can't drain, and your lawn struggles to green up.
  5. Dead patches & bare spots — Between ice, foot traffic, and plow damage, most Madison yards come out of winter with visible bald spots that won't fix themselves.

Spring cleanup isn't optional — it's how you prevent a $500+ lawn renovation later this summer.

What's included in your spring cleanup:

  • Full debris removal (branches, leaves, winter waste)
  • Bed edging and reshaping
  • First mow of the season
  • Gutter check and clearing
  • Typical visit: 2–4 hours depending on property size

We start crews on March 15. Homeowners who book now lock in early bird pricing — 15% off — and get first pick of preferred dates.
emails.review_text: I had them out for a spring clean-up — leaves, branches, lawn tune-up. No upsell garbage, just honest work. It's how service should be.
emails.cta_text: Book Spring Cleanup — Early Bird 15% Off →
emails.urgency_text: Early bird pricing expires March 1. We start crews March 15 — first-booked, first-served.
emails.spotlight_caption: Spring cleanup in Middleton — 3.5 hours from winter wreck to ready for spring.

emails.body_html:
Every spring, the same thing happens: homeowners wait until March to book spring cleanup, then scramble for whatever time slot is left.

This year, {{city}} is booking faster than usual.

Here's what happens when you wait:

  • You get the last available slot — usually a Tuesday afternoon instead of your preferred Saturday morning.
  • Rushed crews — late-season bookings mean we're stacking jobs back-to-back. Early bookers get more time and attention on their property.
  • No crew consistency — when you book early, we assign the same crew to your property all season. Late bookers get whoever is available that day.

Here's what early bookers lock in:

  • ✓ Preferred date and time — morning or afternoon, your choice
  • ✓ Same crew guarantee — same team all season, they learn your property
  • ✓ Early bird 15% discount — only available until March 1
  • ✓ Priority rebooking — if weather delays your date, you move to the front of the reschedule list

Your neighbors aren't waiting. Don't be the last yard on the street still covered in winter debris in April.

emails.hero_image_alt: Beautiful Madison lawn maintained by TotalGuard — lush green grass with clean edges
emails.body_html:
Here's how most homeowners handle spring:

  1. Book a spring cleanup. Wait for it. Done.
  2. Realize the lawn needs mowing. Research companies. Book mowing. Wait.
  3. Neighbor's lawn looks greener. Google "fertilization near me." Book that too.

Three separate bookings. Three separate invoices. Three separate headaches.

Or — one call. One package. An entire season handled.

The Complete Spring Package includes:

✓ Spring Cleanup
Debris removal, bed edging, gutter check, first mow

Consistent cut schedule, same crew every week, edging included

Smart homeowners bundle. They make one decision in March and don't think about their yard again until fall. The lawn stays green, the edges stay sharp, and the neighbors keep asking who they hired.

This package is available through March 31. After that, each service is priced individually — which always costs more.

emails.internal_name: Fall — Fall Is Coming Faster Than You Think
emails.subject_a: Your yard needs these 3 things before the first frost
emails.subject_b: {{first_name}}, the fall prep window is shorter than you think
emails.preview_text: Madison's average first frost: October 8. That gives us 6 weeks.
emails.headline: 3 Things Your Yard Needs Before First Frost
emails.subheadline: Madison's average first frost hits October 8. That gives you roughly 6 weeks to prep your yard — or deal with the consequences in spring.
emails.hero_image_alt: Madison yard with early fall colors — leaves beginning to drop on green lawn
emails.body_html:
Fall prep isn't about raking leaves. It's about setting your yard up to survive winter and come back strong in spring. Skip it, and you'll pay double in March to fix the damage.

Here are the 3 things every Madison yard needs before first frost:

1. Aeration — Let Your Soil Breathe

After a full summer of mowing, foot traffic, and heat, your soil is compacted. Compacted soil suffocates roots and blocks water absorption. Core aeration punches thousands of small holes in your lawn, letting oxygen, water, and nutrients reach the root zone. September is the ideal window — the soil is still warm enough for grass to recover quickly.

2. Overseeding — Repair Summer's Damage

Those bare spots from summer heat, dog traffic, or heavy use? They won't fill in on their own. Overseeding in September gives new grass seed the perfect conditions: warm soil, cooler air, and fall rain. By spring, those patches are filled in and thick. Wait until spring to seed, and you're competing with weeds.

3. Fall Cleanup — Don't Smother Your Grass

Leaves look pretty for about two days. After that, they form a wet mat that blocks sunlight, traps moisture, and breeds snow mold. A proper fall cleanup removes leaves, clears beds, and gives your lawn a clean surface to go dormant under. Yards that skip fall cleanup come out of winter 30% worse.

The window is September through early October. After that, the ground starts freezing and aeration becomes impossible. Our fall schedule is already 30% booked — don't wait until the window closes.
emails.review_text: Vance is responsive and the work is well done. They took on a job that others would not. They are also very quick. They have lots of energy. Highly recommend.
emails.urgency_text: Madison's average first frost: October 8. Our fall schedule is already 30% booked.

emails.internal_name: Fall — The Gutter Problem Nobody Talks About
emails.subject_a: Your gutters are about to become a $3,000 problem
emails.preview_text: 87% of Madison home water damage claims trace back to gutters.
emails.headline: $149 Now or $3,000 Later. Your Choice.
emails.subheadline: Clogged gutters are the #1 cause of preventable water damage in Madison homes. Here's why this fall is the time to fix it.
emails.hero_image_alt: Clogged gutters overflowing with leaves and debris on a Madison home
emails.body_html:
Nobody thinks about their gutters — until water is pooling against their foundation.

Here's the chain reaction that starts with clogged gutters:

Step 1: Leaves and debris clog your gutters through fall.
Cost to fix now: $149
Step 2: Water overflows, runs down your siding, and pools at your foundation.
Cost to repair siding: $500–$800
Step 3: Foundation water seepage leads to basement moisture, mold, and structural cracks.
Cost to repair: $2,000–$5,000+

87% of Madison home water damage claims trace back to gutter issues. That's not a scare tactic — it's insurance data.

Our gutter service includes:

  • Full gutter cleaning — every inch, every downspout, cleared and flushed
  • Downspout check — we verify water flows freely to ground level
  • Gutter inspection — we flag any sagging, loose brackets, or damage
  • Photo documentation — before and after photos sent to your phone

Want to never clean gutters again? Ask about our gutter guard installation — a one-time investment that eliminates seasonal cleaning forever. Most Madison homeowners recoup the cost in 2–3 years of skipped cleanings.

Don't wait for the first hard rain to find out your gutters are clogged. By then, the damage is already happening.
emails.review_text: TotalGuard is the only company I trust with my gutters now. They cleaned mine in the pouring rain and still nailed the job. Zero leaves left, no mess on my siding. Respect.
emails.cta_text: Protect Your Home — Book Gutter Service →
emails.urgency_text: Fall gutter season fills fast — most Madison homeowners book September through October. Schedule now for your preferred date.
emails.spotlight_caption: Gutter cleaning in Waunakee — 45 minutes from clogged to crystal clear.

emails.review_text: Called for a gutter cleaning and ended up hiring them for mowing too. These guys are punctual, friendly, and very detail-oriented. My gutters used to overflow — now they drain perfectly. Worth every penny.

emails.subject_a: Remember shoveling at 5am last February? Never again.
emails.preview_text: Wake up. Driveway clear. Walkway salted. Go to work.
emails.headline: Never. Shovel. Again.
emails.subheadline: Wake up. Driveway clear. Walkway salted. Go to work. That's what snow removal is supposed to feel like.
emails.hero_image_alt: Cleared driveway and salted walkway after a Madison snowfall — clean path to the car
emails.body_html:
Remember last February?

The alarm goes off at 5:00 AM. It's still dark. You check your phone — 6 inches overnight. Your back already hurts just thinking about it.

You layer up. Boots, coat, gloves. Step outside into the wind. Start shoveling. The snow is heavy and wet — the worst kind. Thirty minutes in, you're sweating under three layers, your lower back is screaming, and you've only done half the driveway.

You finish. Walk inside. You're late for work. Your back will hurt for two days. And the next storm is forecast for Thursday.

Now imagine this instead:

Your alarm goes off at 6:30 AM. You look outside — driveway cleared, walkway salted, porch swept. You grab your coffee, walk to your car, and drive to work. You didn't lift a shovel. You didn't even put on boots.

That's TotalGuard snow removal.

How it works:

  • Triggered at 2"+ snowfall — we monitor weather 24/7 and deploy automatically. You never have to call.
  • Driveway + walkways + porch — full clearing, not just a single pass down the middle.
  • Salt/deicer included — applied to all cleared surfaces. No extra charge.
  • 24-hour response — most driveways cleared before 7 AM on heavy snow days.
  • Seasonal contract — one flat rate covers the entire winter. No per-storm surprises.

Your neighbors are already signing up. Madison's snow season runs November through March — that's 5 months of potential snowfall. At an average of 50+ inches per year, that's a lot of mornings you don't want to spend shoveling.
emails.review_text: My lawn looks great. I highly recommend Vance for your lawn care needs. He is conscientious, dependable and hard working.
emails.urgency_text: Early signup pricing available through October 31. After that, rates increase 20%.

emails.subject_b: Your street's snow removal list: are you on it?

emails.headline: {{city}} Is Almost Full.
emails.subheadline: Our snow removal routes have capacity limits. When a route is full, we stop taking signups — no waitlist.
emails.hero_image_alt: Map showing TotalGuard snow removal routes across Madison and surrounding neighborhoods
emails.stakes_html:
Here's how snow removal routing works:

We divide the Madison area into routes. Each route has a maximum number of properties we can service and still guarantee clearing before 7 AM.

When a route fills up, that's it. No waitlist. No exceptions. Adding more properties would mean slower response times for everyone — and we don't do that to our existing customers.

Current route status:
  • Madison West / Middleton — filling fast
  • Waunakee / DeForest — limited spots
  • Monona / McFarland — limited spots
  • Sun Prairie / Cottage Grove — spots available
  • Fitchburg / Verona / Oregon — spots available
Once your area's route is full, the only option is emergency-only service at storm rates — which costs 2–3x the seasonal contract price.
emails.benefits_html:
✓ Automatic dispatch at 2"+ snowfall
✓ Driveway, walkways & porch clearing
✓ Salt/deicer application included
✓ Cleared before 7 AM on most storms
✓ Flat seasonal rate — no per-storm billing
✓ Route priority over non-contract customers
emails.review_text: Very easy to schedule and dependable. Showed up on time and left the yard looking very clean. Worth every dollar.

emails.subheadline: Madison's first measurable snowfall is forecast for next week. After that, seasonal contract pricing disappears.
emails.hero_image_alt: Weather forecast showing incoming snow for the Madison WI area
emails.stakes_html:
The forecast is in. Snow is coming to Madison next week.

This is the last email we'll send before pricing changes. Here's what happens after the first snowfall:

  • Seasonal contract pricing ends immediately — no more flat-rate option
  • Per-storm pricing kicks in — each clearing billed individually at premium rates
  • Emergency-only service — 50% premium over standard per-storm rates
  • No guaranteed response time — contract customers are serviced first, always

The math is simple:

Contract rate (sign up today): One flat price for the entire season. Every storm covered. Priority service guaranteed.

Per-storm rate (after first snow): 25% higher per clearing. No priority. No guarantee we can get to you before you need to leave for work.

Emergency rate (day-of call): 50% premium. First-come, first-served. You might wait 8+ hours.

This is your last chance to lock in the contract rate. Once snow falls, these prices are gone.
